title: Notification Volume and User Annoyance
summary: A study on email and push reminder cadence across dormant and active cohorts. Increasing reminder frequency did not bring dormant users back; instead it raised unsubscribe and mute rates and deepened negative sentiment. Users who received more reminders were more likely to disengage permanently, not less.

quotes.text: The constant reminder emails are exactly why I unsubscribed. It felt like nagging, not helping.
quotes.speaker: Participant 11, lapsed user

quotes.text: More emails made me trust the product less, not more. I muted everything from them.
quotes.speaker: Participant 6, dormant user
